_comment: Shared golden fixtures for the Stencil CLI stderr OUTPUT grammar. Language-neutral
(raw CLI stderr -> expected parsed outcome) cases consumed by BOTH downstream parsers:
so the two ports cannot silently drift. The contract these encode is cli/CONTRACT.md.
Sections map 1:1 to the three parser functions: 'wrote' -> parse_wrote/ParseWrote,
'stderr' is the raw multi-line CLI stderr; 'expected' is the structured result
(null when parse_wrote finds no success line; a possibly-empty list for remotes).

wrote.name: wrote with px + page-format dims tail
wrote line found among banner / usage noise
wrote.stderr:
___ stencil banner ___
some usage text
wrote out.png (16x12)

wrote.name: path containing a parenthesis (rfind on last ' (')
any page-format label in the suffix (B5), still informational

remotes.name: --remote-update writes back into the fetched project
remotes.stderr:
wrote out.png (800x600)
updated server result for project p_x_y (800x600)

remotes.name: --remote creates a new project (quoted name, parenthesised id)
remotes.stderr:
wrote out.png (16x12)
created server project "My Shot" (p_a_b)

remotes.name: both deliveries in one run, in order
remotes.stderr:
wrote out.png (10x10)
updated server result for project p_1 (10x10)
created server project "Copy" (p_2)

errors.name: single error line among banner + usage
errors.stderr:
banner
usage: stencil ...
error: could not parse --crop "oops"

errors.name: multiple error lines joined with newline, in order
errors.stderr:
error: refusing to fetch internal/blocked host 'x'
error: HTTP 404 fetching http://y
errors.expected:
error: refusing to fetch internal/blocked host 'x'
error: HTTP 404 fetching http://y

errors.name: no error: prefix falls back to whole trimmed stderr
empty stderr yields a generic message
errors.expected: the stencil CLI failed without a message
